purpose: Tracks the source/channel attribution for each signup: organic search, affiliate referral, college partnership, etc. Normalized to enable multiple attributions per signup and detailed analytics.

important_fields.name: id
important_fields.note: Unique identifier for each attribution record.

important_fields.name: signup_id
important_fields.note: Reference to the signup record. Multiple rows per signup_id allowed (e.g., both 'organic' and 'college_referral' if user found via both channels).

important_fields.name: source_type
important_fields.note: Classification of the referral channel. Enables filtering signups by acquisition source for success metrics and ROI analysis.

important_fields.name: source_identifier
important_fields.note: Additional identifier for the source: affiliate partner name, college referral_code, UTM campaign, custom promo code, etc. Enables detailed attribution per partner.

important_fields.name: utm_source
important_fields.note: UTM parameter (e.g., 'google', 'facebook'). Captured from referral link if available.

important_fields.name: utm_medium
important_fields.note: UTM parameter (e.g., 'cpc', 'social', 'email'). Provides additional channel context.

important_fields.name: utm_campaign
important_fields.note: UTM parameter (e.g., 'jee_prep_launch'). Tracks specific campaigns or initiatives.

important_fields.name: created_at
important_fields.note: Timestamp when the attribution was recorded (usually same as signup.created_at unless backfilled).

relationships.note: Each signup_sources row references exactly one signup. Multiple sources per signup are allowed.

notes: This normalized table enables flexible attribution tracking. For example, if a student finds the website via organic search but then clicks a college referral link, both sources can be recorded. The signup form or referral link should pass source metadata (source_type, source_identifier, UTM params) to the backend, which creates signup_sources records. This table is essential for measuring success metrics: 'trial signup rate from college referrals vs. organic', 'affiliate ROI', etc. College-referred signups are tracked by setting source_type='college_referral' and source_identifier to the college's referral_code.
